frameworks.name: high-definition transcranial direct current stimulation (HD-tDCS)
frameworks.one_line: focal non-invasive cortical stimulation via small HD electrodes over V5

frameworks.name: noise-reduction account of cathodal stimulation
frameworks.one_line: cathodal decreases global excitation, suppressing sub-threshold noise

frameworks.name: lesion-based visual associative cortex framework
frameworks.one_line: associative-cortex lesions impair visual perception, motivating V5 targeting

core_question: Does cathodal or anodal HD-tDCS over right V5 modulate motion and shape perception in healthy adults?
key_assumptions: HD electrodes deliver more focal cortical current than sponge electrodes
right V5 is a causal node for contralateral motion perception
sham stimulation provides a valid perceptual baseline
manipulations: stimulation polarity (cathodal vs anodal vs sham)
key_findings: cathodal HD-tDCS improved motion perception in the left hemifield
shape perception was unaffected by cathodal stimulation
anodal and sham HD-tDCS did not change performance
interpretations: cathodal HD-tDCS may sharpen perception by lowering sub-threshold cortical noise
polarity-specific, domain-specific effect localised to right V5
limitations_noted: single-blind within-subject design with a small healthy sample
